portfolio_rationale: LLY stands out as the highest-conviction pick with consistent bullish signals, strong revenue growth (36%), positive price momentum above all key moving averages, and a MACD above signal — the bull case clearly dominates. GOOGL earns a meaningful allocation due to deeply oversold RSI (25.7), strong balance sheet (minimal debt, current ratio 1.9), and a weak bear case relying only on short-term momentum. The remaining bullish names (AAPL, BRK.B, XOM) receive small allocations given mixed signal histories, weaker technical setups, and less differentiated bull cases, while AMZN is excluded on a bearish judge signal.

LLY.reason: Strongest bull case: consistent 3-week bullish history, 36% revenue growth, price above all MAs, MACD above signal, and bear case rebutted convincingly on leverage concerns.

GOOGL.reason: Deeply oversold RSI (25.7) with strong fundamentals (ROE 26%, minimal debt, current ratio 1.9) creates compelling rebound setup; bear case relies only on short-term momentum.

AAPL.reason: Judge bullish at 0.85 but 3-week neutral history and price below 20-day MA reduce conviction; strong profitability provides a floor.

BRK.B.reason: Solid fundamentals and positive news sentiment support the bull case, but negative revenue growth and mixed signal history (bullish/bearish/bullish) limit conviction.

XOM.reason: Weakest bull case among bullish signals — thin fundamental support, price below 20-day MA, negative 5d and 20d momentum, and mostly neutral recent signal history.

AMZN.reason: Judge signal is bearish; excluded from portfolio.
